response:
to emulate the delete confirmation page behavior before saving certain models in the admin, you can implement a mechanism that checks for dependencies and triggers a confirmation dialog when an object is modified. this can be done by adding a custom method in the parent model that handles the cascading deletion logic. the method can check if any dependent objects are out-of-date and prompt the user for confirmation before proceeding with the save.

the key is to separate the confirmation logic from the save method, so that it can be easily tested and maintained. in django, you can use the `save` method of the parent model to trigger the confirmation, and if the user declines, you can rollback the changes using a try-except block. this approach ensures that the user is prompted for confirmation before any irreversible actions are taken, providing a more intuitive user experience.
